causalAutopsy.notes: Preventive gate for a LATENT class: synchronous process-enumeration scans (ps/pgrep/lsof) on a cadence were always present in monitors but dormant — they only became harmful on 2026-06-07 (topic 21816, docs/postmortems/2026-06-07-server-temporarily-down.md, root cause #4) when CPU/IO load made them run long enough to block the single-threaded event loop and starve /health, so the supervisor judged an alive server unresponsive and restarted it -> the restart loop. PR #972 converted the worst offender (SessionWatchdog) to async; this lint is the Structure>Willpower ratchet that stops the latent class from being re-introduced. Not a regression of a prior PR — a preventive gate closing the door behind the fix.
